title: Add Qwen Code as a new external CLI agent
prompt:
Add support for Qwen Code (the `qwen` CLI, Alibaba's Gemini-CLI fork) as a new external CLI agent option in Kanban, alongside the existing claude/codex/droid/kiro/gemini/opencode agents.

Scope — follow the existing pattern for an external CLI agent end to end:
- Register the new agent id (e.g. `qwen`) in the agent catalog at src/core/agent-catalog.ts, including display name, binary name, and any icon/metadata the other CLI agents carry.
- Wire it into the RuntimeAgentId enum / agent contract in src/core/api-contract.ts so the web-ui shared type (@runtime-contract alias) picks it up; run web:typecheck to confirm no ripple breaks.
- Add binary detection (direct PATH check, NOT an interactive shell — see AGENTS.md tribal note on shell init) and task-agent startup/launch wiring matching how gemini/opencode are launched, including any session-id / resume flags Qwen Code supports.
- Verify the terminal session transcript capture path works for the new agent (turn boundary via the session state machine), mirroring the other CLI agents.
- Add the agent to any web-ui agent selector / picker so it is user-selectable.
- Add/extend tests where the other CLI agents are tested.

Reference: Qwen Code is a Gemini-CLI-derived terminal coding agent; its integration should closely mirror the existing `gemini` agent. Inspect how `gemini` and `opencode` are added across catalog, contract, detection, startup, and web-ui, and replicate for `qwen`. Confirm the exact `qwen` binary name and supported CLI flags before wiring startup.
